content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 897 • SACRAMENTO, 95814 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

April 15, 1976

Assemblyman Paul Priolo
California Legislature
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA. 91814

Dear Assemblyman Priolo:

Thank you for your letter of April 2, 1976, seeking
advice with respect to the reporting requirements created by
reason of a speech you delivered to the Fresno Republican
Associates. You indicate, in your letter, that you received
transportation via a private plane to and from Fresno and
that your hotel and dinner expenses were paid.

Pursuant to a regulation adopted by the Commission,
2 Cal. Adm. Code §18728, the payment for your air fare and
the dinner in connection with the event at which you spoke is
not a "payment" within the meaning of the Political Reform
Act, and, therefore, no reporting obligation is incurred by
reason of such payment. The payment of a hotel bill, on the
other hand, can be either a gift or income pursuant to the
aforementioned regulation and, thus, could create a reporting
obligation. However, under the circumstances you describe,
your speech undoubtedly represented equal or greater value
than the amount of the hotel bill, and, therefore, the amount
of the hotel bill would be income and would be reportable
only if it were $250 or more. Since it is doubtful that the
hotel at which you stayed in Fresno charged $250 for your
room, you did not incur any reporting obligations by reason
of your activities in connection with the speech you
delivered.

If you have any further questions, please do not
hesitate to contact Kenneth Finney, Chief of the Commission's
Legal Division.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

DHL:pvp

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5601 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441
